articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Victor Boniface (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the left side of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Álex Grimaldo.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Jeremie Frimpong (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Aleix García with a through ball.
Dennis Geiger (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Emiliano Buendía replaces Nathan Tella because of an injury.
Álex Grimaldo (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
First Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, TSG Hoffenheim 0.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Patrik Schick replaces Victor Boniface.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, TSG Hoffenheim 0. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the top left corner following a corner.
Second yellow card to Álex Grimaldo (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) for a bad foul.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Gift Orban replaces Dennis Geiger.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Stanley Nsoki replaces Marius Bülter.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Hennes Behrens replaces David Jurásek.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, TSG Hoffenheim 1. Gift Orban (TSG Hoffenheim) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal following a set piece situation.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Mario Hermoso replaces Emiliano Buendía.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. David Mokwa replaces Robin Hranác.
Erencan Yardimci (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card.
Jonathan Tah (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Tom Bischof (TSG Hoffenheim) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Exequiel Palacios replaces Florian Wirtz.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Arthur replaces Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, TSG Hoffenheim. Alexander Prass replaces Erencan Yardimci.
Aleix García (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card.
Second Half ends, Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, TSG Hoffenheim 1.

article.story:
Bayer Leverkusen earned a 3-1 home win over relegation-threatened Hoffenheim on Sunday to remain six points off Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich.

The German champions bounced back after last week's disappointing 2-2 draw at RB Leipzig and sit second on 45 points. Hoffenheim remain 15th on 18 points, four above the drop zone.

Leverkusen opened the scoring after 15 minutes when Victor Boniface, returning from injury, met Alejandro Grimaldo's pass in the box and struck home at the far post, beating goalkeeper Luca Philipp.

Jeremie Frimpong doubled the lead five minutes later, coolly finding the bottom far corner after Aleix Garcia's fine through ball and substitute Patrik Schick made it 3-0 in the 51st minute with a superb shot into the top right corner.

"Victor was injured for a long time and hit the target again straight away," Schick said of the Nigerian striker. "I know myself how difficult that is. It's also very important for him that he scored again straight away."

Leverkusen thought they would have a chance to increase their lead before the break when they were awarded a penalty, but it was reversed by VAR, with the referee announcing the decision to fans over the stadium speakers, for the first time in the Bundesliga.

While the hosts pushed to extend their lead, their momentum took a hit on the hour mark when they were reduced to 10 men after Grimaldo received a second yellow card for a foul on Marius Bulter.

Gift Orban, who had just come on, pulled one back for Hoffenheim in the 62nd minute with a close-range finish, but the visitors failed to capitalise on their numerical advantage to mount a comeback. 

Leverkusen, who last lost in the league in August, host Cologne in the German Cup quarter-finals on Wednesday.

"Of course we would have preferred to have won 3-0," Leverkusen defender Jonathan Tah said. "But, overall, it was a good game from us. Now we're looking forward to Wednesday."
